meta_title: Impact Doors in Roosevelt Gardens, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs HVHZ-rated impact doors in Roosevelt Gardens, FL. ESW & CWI brands, 170mph wind protection, financing from $87/mo. Call today!
h1: Roosevelt Gardens' Most Trusted Impact Door Installation — Built for 170mph Winds and Broward's Strictest Standards
intro:
Roosevelt Gardens is a tight-knit Broward County community where generations of families have put down roots, built equity, and weathered serious storms together. With average home values around $240,000 and roughly 65% of the housing stock built before 1994, the neighborhood carries the charm of an established South Florida community — along with the vulnerability that comes with aging entry points. If your home's front door, sliding glass door, or French doors were installed before the modern era of impact-resistant technology, they may not meet today's High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) standards, leaving your property, your family, and your investment dangerously exposed.

Located approximately 4.5 miles from the Atlantic coastline and sitting squarely within Broward County's 170mph design wind speed zone, Roosevelt Gardens doesn't get the luxury of dismissing hurricane preparedness as a coastal concern. Wind, rain, and storm surge don't stop at the shore — and the ten named storms that have impacted this region since 1990 are proof of that. Hurricanes Andrew, Wilma, and Irma each left their mark on Broward communities just like this one, exposing homes with substandard doors and windows to devastating wind-driven rain infiltration, forced entry from debris impact, and catastrophic structural damage. The right impact doors aren't a luxury upgrade here — they're a necessity.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, known throughout South Florida as "The Kings of Service," we specialize in bringing Roosevelt Gardens homeowners the gold standard in impact door protection. Whether you're upgrading a pre-1994 home on a tree-lined block near Roosevelt Gardens Park or reinforcing a newer construction that still has standard doors, our team brings over 60 years of combined experience directly to your doorstep. We offer premium ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI impact door systems — entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors — all engineered specifically for Florida's HVHZ designation and installed 100% in-house by our own licensed craftsmen.

section_why.h2: Why Roosevelt Gardens Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Impact Door Installation
section_why.body:
Choosing the right contractor for impact door installation in Roosevelt Gardens isn't just about price — it's about trust, quality, and the knowledge that your home will be protected when the next storm rolls through Broward County. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built a reputation across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties as the company that does the job right the first time, every time. With an A+ BBB Rating, over 500 completed homes, and a team of installers who are all direct employees — never subcontractors — we bring a level of accountability and consistency that other companies simply can't match.

What sets us apart in Roosevelt Gardens specifically is our deep familiarity with the Broward County Building Division's permitting process and the nuances of HVHZ compliance. Our project managers understand local code requirements inside and out, which means faster permit approvals, fewer inspection surprises, and installations that pass on the first walkthrough. Many of our clients in nearby communities like Fort Lauderdale, Lauderhill, and Lauderdale Lakes have come to us after bad experiences with fly-by-night contractors who hired unlicensed subs and disappeared when problems arose. With Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you get a single point of contact from the initial quote through the final inspection.

Our 60-plus years of combined installation experience means our crews have seen every type of home construction in this part of Broward — from the older CBS (concrete block structure) homes built in the 1960s and 70s that dominate Roosevelt Gardens to mid-century wood-frame structures that require special framing considerations during door installation. We adapt our process to your home's specific construction type, ensuring a weathertight, code-compliant seal every time. Every installation includes multi-point locking systems for both security and storm performance, and our work is fully licensed and insured for your protection.

Financially, we understand that Roosevelt Gardens is a working community where homeowners are careful about where their dollars go. That's why we offer flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down, making it possible to upgrade your home's protection without draining your savings. Investing in HVHZ-rated impact doors is one of the highest-ROI home improvements available in South Florida — and our team is here to make it as accessible as possible for every homeowner in the neighborhood.

section_history.h2: Storms That Shaped Roosevelt Gardens: Why Impact Door Protection Is Non-Negotiable Here
section_history.body:
Roosevelt Gardens and the broader Broward County region have been tested repeatedly by major Atlantic hurricanes, and the lessons from each storm have underscored the same hard truth: standard doors are no match for South Florida's weather. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 — before modern HVHZ building codes were enacted — caused catastrophic damage across Broward and Miami-Dade, tearing through homes with standard entry doors and sliding glass panels as if they were made of cardboard. That storm changed Florida building codes forever and is the reason the HVHZ designation exists today. Yet thousands of homes in Roosevelt Gardens were already built by then, and many still carry those pre-Andrew vulnerabilities.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 struck as a powerful Category 3 system and raked directly across Broward County, causing widespread power outages, roof damage, and window and door failures throughout communities from Fort Lauderdale to Lauderhill. Homeowners who had invested in impact products fared dramatically better than neighbors relying on shutters and standard doors — many of whom returned to find water-logged interiors, damaged hardwood floors, and ruined furniture from wind-driven rain that forced its way through compromised door frames and seals. Irma in 2017, while slightly weaker at landfall, served as another reminder that no Broward community is immune to serious storm damage.

With ten named storms impacting this region since 1990, the data is clear: Roosevelt Gardens homeowners face a recurring, credible hurricane threat. Each storm season brings renewed urgency to the question of whether your home's doors — particularly the large sliding glass doors and entry doors that are most vulnerable to wind pressure differentials — are truly built to withstand what's coming. Impact doors rated for 170mph wind loads don't just prevent the dramatic movie-scene blowout; they resist the relentless pressure cycling and water infiltration that causes the hidden, cumulative damage that devalues homes and triggers costly insurance claims year after year.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code and HVHZ Requirements for Impact Doors in Roosevelt Gardens
section_code.body:
Roosevelt Gardens falls entirely within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone, which represents the most demanding building code jurisdiction in the entire United States. The HVHZ designation — which applies to Broward and Miami-Dade counties — mandates that all exterior openings, including entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors, meet a minimum design wind speed of 170mph for large missile impact testing. This is not the same standard applied in other parts of Florida or the country; it is a significantly elevated requirement born directly from the destruction wrought by Hurricane Andrew and decades of post-storm engineering research.

When Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact doors in Roosevelt Gardens, every product we use carries the Florida Product Approval (FPA) necessary for HVHZ compliance, and every installation is permitted through the Broward County Building Division. The permitting process in Broward requires detailed product specifications, installation drawings, and NOA (Notice of Acceptance) documentation from Miami-Dade's rigorous testing program — the gold standard in impact product certification. Our team manages all of this paperwork on your behalf, ensuring your installation is fully documented and legally compliant before our crews ever arrive at your door.

For homeowners with pre-1994 construction — which accounts for roughly 65% of Roosevelt Gardens' housing stock — code compliance upgrades aren't just about safety; they're frequently required when selling a home or making significant renovations. Broward County inspectors will verify that all installed impact doors meet current HVHZ standards during the final inspection. Non-compliant installations can result in failed inspections, required removal and replacement at the homeowner's expense, and complications with insurance claims. Choosing a contractor like Premier Impact Windows & Roofing who knows the Broward County Building Division's requirements inside and out protects you from all of these costly pitfalls.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Protection Engineered for Roosevelt Gardens Homes
section_products.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we install two of the most respected impact door product lines available in South Florida: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands are engineered specifically for Florida's HVHZ requirements and carry Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) approvals — the most rigorous product certification standard in the country. Whether you're replacing a worn single-panel entry door on a 1970s CBS home near Roosevelt Gardens Park or upgrading to a sweeping French door system on a renovated property, these product lines offer the performance, aesthetics, and long-term durability that South Florida homeowners need.

ESW impact entry doors are built with heavy-gauge steel or fiberglass skins and feature multi-point locking systems that engage at three or more points along the door frame simultaneously — a critical feature in HVHZ environments where wind pressure can exploit even minor frame weaknesses. Their impact-laminated glass inserts provide both storm protection and natural light without sacrificing the clean, finished look that adds curb appeal and resale value to your home. CWI's product line complements ESW with an exceptional range of sliding glass impact doors and French door systems, offering superior thermal performance, smooth operation under repeated use, and the structural integrity to withstand sustained 170mph wind loads and large-missile impact testing.

Both ESW and CWI products are designed with Roosevelt Gardens' climate in mind — not just hurricanes, but the year-round exposure to intense UV radiation, salt-laden air from proximity to the coast, and the thermal cycling that causes cheaper materials to warp, seal, and seize over time. The frames, hardware, and glazing compounds used in these systems are selected for long-term performance in South Florida's demanding environment, meaning your impact doors will look and function like new for years after installation. Combined with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's meticulous in-house installation process, these products deliver the full measure of protection your home deserves.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Doors Cost in Roosevelt Gardens — and How Financing Makes It Work for You
section_cost.body:
For Roosevelt Gardens homeowners with a median home value around $240,000, the investment in impact doors needs to make financial sense — and the good news is that it does, in multiple measurable ways. The cost of impact door installation in Broward County typically ranges from $1,200 to $4,500 per door depending on the product type, size, and complexity of installation. A standard impact entry door upgrade falls on the lower end of that range, while large sliding glass door systems or decorative French door configurations with sidelites land higher. Most Roosevelt Gardens homeowners completing a full door upgrade — front entry, back sliding glass, and secondary entries — invest between $4,000 and $10,000 total, depending on the scope of work.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes that investment accessible with flexible financing options that require $0 down and start as low as $87 per month. For a community where budgeting matters, the ability to spread impact door costs over time while immediately enjoying the safety, energy efficiency, and insurance benefits is a genuine game-changer. We work with financing partners who specialize in home improvement lending and can often provide same-day approvals, so you don't have to wait weeks to get your project started. Our team will walk you through every financing option during your free in-home estimate, with no pressure and no hidden fees.

From a pure ROI perspective, impact doors in Roosevelt Gardens deliver returns that most home improvements can't touch. First, you'll see immediate reductions on your homeowner's insurance premium — often 10% to 30% or more depending on your current carrier and coverage level — which directly offsets your monthly financing payment. Second, impact doors improve energy efficiency by reducing air infiltration and solar heat gain, which lowers your FPL cooling costs during South Florida's long summer season. Third, in a market where buyers increasingly expect impact protection, upgraded doors add meaningful resale value to your home, making them one of the smartest improvements you can make on a property in the $240,000 range.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Roosevelt Gardens Homeowners Who Upgrade to Impact Doors
section_insurance.body:
One of the most compelling financial arguments for installing impact doors in Roosevelt Gardens is the substantial savings available through Broward County homeowner's insurance carriers. Florida insurers are required by law to offer discounts for homes with verified opening protection, and in the HVHZ, where the risk profile is highest, those discounts are correspondingly significant. Homeowners who complete a full opening protection upgrade — including impact-rated doors on all exterior openings — typically qualify for the maximum wind mitigation credits available under Florida's Uniform Mitigation Verification Inspection (UMVI) program, which can reduce annual wind coverage premiums by hundreds or even thousands of dollars.

The process begins with a wind mitigation inspection conducted by a licensed inspector after your impact doors are installed and permitted. The inspector documents the product approvals and installation quality, and that report is submitted to your insurance carrier to trigger the applicable discounts. Common carriers operating in Broward County — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Insurance, and others — all use the Florida UMVI form as the basis for their wind mitigation rating, so your Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installation creates a documented, transferable record of protection that carries value for years. In fact, when you sell your home, a valid wind mitigation report with full opening protection can be a significant marketing asset to buyers who are acutely aware of Florida's insurance costs.

Beyond the direct premium discounts, impact doors also reduce your exposure to deductible risk. Most Florida homeowner's policies carry a separate hurricane deductible of 2% to 5% of insured value — on a $240,000 Roosevelt Gardens home, that's $4,800 to $12,000 out of pocket before insurance pays a single dollar in a storm claim. By preventing wind and water intrusion at the entry point level, impact doors dramatically reduce the likelihood you'll ever have to write that check. When you factor in avoided claims, lower premiums, and energy savings together, the financial case for impact doors in Roosevelt Gardens is overwhelming.

faqs.q: Do Roosevelt Gardens homes really need HVHZ-rated impact doors, even though we're 4.5 miles from the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. The High Velocity Hurricane Zone designation applies to all of Broward County regardless of distance from the coast. Roosevelt Gardens sits in a 170mph design wind speed zone, meaning every exterior opening — including all doors — must meet HVHZ standards to be code-compliant. Inland homes are just as exposed to storm-force winds and flying debris as coastal properties; the HVHZ requirement exists precisely because post-storm data showed that wind damage doesn't diminish significantly over 4 to 5 miles of inland distance.

faqs.q: How much does it cost to install impact doors in Roosevelt Gardens, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Impact door installation in Roosevelt Gardens typically ranges from $1,200 to $4,500 per door depending on size and style, with most full-home upgrades falling between $4,000 and $10,000. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down, and our financing partners often provide same-day approvals. When you factor in insurance savings that can reach hundreds of dollars per year and energy cost reductions from improved air sealing, most homeowners see a strong return on investment within a few years of installation.

faqs.q: What permits are required to install impact doors in Broward County, and does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handle that process?
faqs.a: Yes, impact door installations in Broward County require a permit from the Broward County Building Division, along with product specifications, installation drawings, and Miami-Dade NOA (Notice of Acceptance) documentation proving HVHZ compliance. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting process on your behalf — from submitting the application to coordinating the final inspection. Our team is thoroughly familiar with Broward's requirements, which means faster approvals and installations that pass inspection the first time.

faqs.q: How long does the impact door installation process take in Roosevelt Gardens?
faqs.a: From the time you sign your contract, most Roosevelt Gardens impact door projects are completed within 4 to 8 weeks. This timeline includes permit submission and approval through the Broward County Building Division (typically 2 to 4 weeks), product fabrication and delivery, and the actual installation — which typically takes one to two days for a full home door upgrade. Our project manager will keep you informed throughout the process, and our 100% in-house installation crews are scheduled based on your availability.

faqs.q: Will installing impact doors lower my homeowner's insurance in Roosevelt Gardens?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. After your impact doors are installed and permitted, a licensed wind mitigation inspector will document the upgrade using Florida's Uniform Mitigation Verification form. That report is submitted to your insurance carrier to unlock wind mitigation discounts that can reduce annual premiums by 10% to 30% or more depending on your carrier and the scope of your opening protection. On a Roosevelt Gardens home insured for $240,000, those savings can be substantial and accumulate year after year for as long as you own the home.
